header_summary:
Recent discussions in the Bitcoin development community have highlighted several innovative proposals aimed at enhancing the security and functionality of the Bitcoin network. The Hourglass proposal, introduced to mitigate risks associated with mass liquidation of P2PK funds, has been revised to limit the Bitcoin amount that can be spent from such outputs to one Bitcoin per block, aiming to bolster network stability. Details of the revised proposal and its technical adjustments are documented on [GitHub](https://github.com/cryptoquick/bips/blob/hourglass-v2/bip-hourglass-v2.mediawiki).

In parallel, advancements in the bitcoin backbone have been shared, focusing on support for compact-block BIP152, improvements in transaction synchronization, and the introduction of a custom C interface for enhanced interaction with the consensus code. These updates are part of ongoing efforts to refine the system's reliability and efficiency, with the potential for further enhancements discussed among developers, including interface extensions for improved blockchain data interaction and mempool management strategies. More information can be found [here](http://bitcoinbackbone.org).

Moreover, the Silent Payments module for libsecp256k1 has been scrutinized for performance issues with BIP-352, leading to the proposal of a "K_max" protocol limit to address inefficiencies in transactions with a large number of recipients sharing the same scan public key. This adjustment aims to maintain the functionality of current Silent Payment wallets while managing adversarial transactions more effectively. The dialogue on this proposal continues, with further details available in the proposal draft on [GitHub](https://github.com/theStack/bips/commit/961d1442139ceecd6c0cc5775ef911d69aabed4c).

Additionally, the Boomerang protocol introduces a duress mechanism for enhanced user security, enabling discreet distress signaling during coercive situations without alerting the attacker. This sophisticated system relies on encrypted communications and partnerships with Search and Rescue entities, utilizing a user-created "consent set" of countries for duress checks. The protocol aims to provide robust security features while ensuring plausible deniability for users under duress, detailed further [here](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/boomerang-bitcoin-cold-storage-with-built-in-coercion-resistance/2239/2).

recent_posts.title: Hourglass V2 Update
recent_posts.summary:
- The Hourglass proposal has been revised to limit Bitcoin spending to one per block.
- Detailed information on the revised proposal is available on GitHub for further exploration.
- Community feedback is sought to refine and enhance the proposal's effectiveness.

recent_posts.title: New bitcoin backbone code release: BIP152, outbound tx support + libbitcoinkernel API feedbacks
recent_posts.summary:
- The update enhances bitcoin backbone with compact-block support and other improvements.
- A custom C interface now improves interaction with consensus code for flexibility.
- Suggestions for new interface extensions focus on better blockchain data interaction and transaction validation.

recent_posts.title: BIP-352: Limiting the number of per-group recipients (K_max)
recent_posts.summary:
- The Silent Payments module's scanning method faces performance challenges with adversarial transactions.
- A new "K_max" protocol limit proposes restricting recipients per group in transactions.
- Community feedback is sought via GitHub to refine this Bitcoin Improvement Proposal.

recent_posts.title: Hourglass V2 Update
recent_posts.summary:
- Hourglass introduces a flag day soft fork to address P2PK coin security, incentivizing early transfers.
- It offers a novel method for asset reclamation post-activation, distinguishing between genuine holders and quantum attackers.
- The proposal suggests a decrease in P2PK transactions post-activation will secure the blockchain against quantum threats.

recent_posts.title: Algorithm agility to defeat quantum and classical attacks on Bitcoin's signature algorithms
recent_posts.summary:
- The proposal suggests enhancing Bitcoin's security by introducing algorithm agility to counter long-term threats.
- A new post-quantum signature algorithm would be added for emergency use, ensuring Bitcoin's value and security for up to 75 years.
- The implementation plan includes supporting two digital signature algorithms and introducing BIP 360 for integrating a hash-based signature scheme.

recent_posts.title: Boomerang: Bitcoin Cold Storage with Built-In Coercion Resistance
recent_posts.summary: - The Boomerang protocol features a duress mechanism, enhancing security via a "consent set" of five countries.  - It employs encrypted signals and SAR partnerships for timely rescues, triggered by deviations in user's country selections.  - Designed to thwart replay and observation attacks, it maintains security even under coercion, keeping user actions indiscernible.

active_posts.title: Addressing remaining points on BIP 54
active_posts.summary:
- BIP 54 discussion reignites, focusing on pros and cons of embedding extranonce in nLockTime.
- Jeremy Rubin proposes a sparse Merkle tree to avoid smart contract issues, facing skepticism.
- Despite concerns, consensus leans towards pragmatic solutions avoiding major protocol changes.

active_posts.title: Algorithm Agility for Bitcoin to maintain security in the face of quantum and classic breaks in the signature algorithms
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal enhances Bitcoin's security against quantum computing and algorithm breaks.
- It introduces a dual signature system with primary and emergency backup algorithms.
- This approach aims to maintain Bitcoin's reliability as a long-term investment option.

active_posts.title: Bip54: add sequence, timestamp (and version) to GBT
active_posts.summary:
- BIP54 focuses on setting `nLockTime` and `nSequence` in coinbase transactions by mining pools.
- It introduces keys in `getblocktemplate` for better future soft fork compatibility.
- The proposal underscores compliance importance for future-proofing against protocol changes.

active_posts.title: Gossip Observer: New project to monitor the Lightning P2P network
active_posts.summary:
- The project monitors Lightning Network gossip, sharing code and data findings online.
- Observations indicate reduced message spread time and potential network inefficiencies.
- Future plans include expanding infrastructure and inviting community contribution to the analysis.

active_posts.title: Major BIP 360 Update
active_posts.summary:
- Ethan Heilman and colleagues have rewritten BIP 360 for clarity and quantum-risk mitigation.
- The update introduces "Pay-to-Tapscript-Hash" to enhance Bitcoin's quantum resistance without post-quantum schemes.
- New Python and Rust test vectors are included, with invites for community review at BIP Repo and BIP360.org.

active_posts.title: Benchmarking Bitcoin Script Evaluation for the Varops Budget (Great Script Restoration)
active_posts.summary:
- A new Tapscript leaf version aims to mitigate denial-of-service attacks by modifying the opcode system.
- The varops budget, linked to transaction weight, allows for proportional compute units, with tests for efficiency.
- Community feedback on the prototype and benchmarks is sought to refine and ensure the varops budget's effectiveness.

today_in_history_posts.title: BIP for deterministic pay-to-script-hash multi-signature addresses
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- A new BIP drafts deterministic multi-signature addresses via public key sorting.
- The BIP ensures consistent P2SH addresses for multisig accounts by standardizing key sorting.
- It mentions PGP for authentication but details on secure communication remain unclear.

today_in_history_posts.title: Routing and explicit trust
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The paper questions the safety of routing through "channel counterparty" and "clearinghouse." - It highlights the challenge in trusting counterparties and selecting intermediaries in networks.
- Discusses risks of systemic failures and interconnected counterparty exposures in banking versus protocols.

today_in_history_posts.title: Highly Available Lightning Channels Revisited – ROUTE OR OUT
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- A new strategy aims to improve Lightning Network transactions by bypassing failing nodes.
- High Availability (HA) channels might attract higher fees for their reliability in transactions.
- Developers debate the practicality of the proposed changes and their impact on network dynamics.
